name: Tropical Puff Pancake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Place oil in a 10-inch ovenproof skillet; heat in the oven until hot, about 10 to 12 minutes.
2. In a small bowl, combine flour, baking powder, and salt with eggs; beat at medium speed until well blended. Gradually stir in milk and melted margarine (or butter). Pour batter into the hot skillet.
3. Bake at 425°F for 15 minutes. Reduce oven temperature to 375°F. Bake an additional 10 to 12 minutes, or until puffed and golden brown. A well will form in the center and edges will puff up.
4. In a small saucepan, combine cornstarch and pineapple tidbits. Cook over medium-low heat until slightly thickened, stirring occasionally. Fold in coconut pieces and banana slices. Remove pancake from oven; spoon filling into the center. Serve immediately. (The pancake may collapse as it cools.) It also works well with apple slices and cinnamon.
